Backtest trailing orders on Hyperliquid

Compare a fixed stop-loss with trailing stop-loss, take-profit, or limit-entry orders on live Delta Lab price data, for a trade taken at the start of the window.

Installing this path wires up a Claude skill: for limit orders and trailing entries you'll be prompted before the order is placed; for market orders you'll be prompted after the fill, so the entry isn't delayed.

How each kind works
  • Trailing stop-loss. Armed from tick 1. The peak tracks the favorable extreme (high for longs, low for shorts); a pullback of Primary % closes the trade at market.
  • Trailing take-profit. Dormant until the trade is ahead by Primary % (the activation gate — no order on the exchange yet). Once activated, it arms a resting trigger that trails the peak by If price reverses by — i.e. post-activation it behaves exactly like a trailing SL. Keep the reversal smaller than your SL offset so the TP fires first on tight pullbacks.
  • Trailing limit entry. No position yet; the peak tracks the adverse extreme. Fires a market entry once price reverses by If price reverses by off that extreme.

Type a Hyperliquid symbol, pick an order type, and slide the percentage to see how the outcomes diverge.
